info.parenttitle: A3.3 Introduction to mechanical systems (HL only)

markdown:
## Linkages


<definition term="Linkage">A **mechanical system** of **connected parts** that **changes the direction** or **size of a force**.</definition>

**Linkages** are essential components in **mechanical systems**, used to:

1. Change the direction of movement
2. Alter the magnitude of a force
3. Control the motion of system parts

### Types of Linkages


  1. Parallel Linkages
  2. Reverse Linkages
  3. Bell Crank Linkages

#### Parallel Linkages


<definition term="Parallel Linkage">A mechanism where a **central point is constrained to move in a nearly straight line** by the arrangement of **linked bars**.</definition>

1. **Maintains Orientation**: Keeps the connected parts parallel throughout the motion.
2. **Even Force Distribution**: Distributes force evenly across the linkage.

![Parallel Linkage (BBC)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/854aa32e55ecca624328725b20b9b3f3693df195-976x549.png)

<callout type="example">* **Suspension Systems**: Used in vehicles to keep wheels aligned with the road.
* **Drawing Boards**: Ensures the drawing surface remains level.</callout>

#### Reverse Linkages


<definition term="Reverse Linkage">A **linkage** that **reverses the direction** of a **force or motion**.</definition>

1. **Direction Change**: Converts input motion into the opposite output motion.
2. **Simple Design**: Often consists of a single pivot point.

![Reverse Linkage (BBC)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/1e693963c1aa6d58b4552ea8e223062b9fd4a8a0-976x549.png)

<callout type="example">* **Windshield Wipers**: Converts rotational motion into back-and-forth movement.
* **Pumps**: Changes linear motion into rotational motion.</callout>

### Bell Crank Linkages


<definition term="Bell Crank Linkage">A **linkage** that **converts vertical motion** into **horizontal motion**, or the other way around.</definition>

1. **Angle Conversion**: Alters force direction, usually at a right angle.
2. **Force Amplification**: Can increase or decrease force magnitude.

![Bell Crank Linkage (BBC)](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/333d830fd75b65eb60fc29c2284741dc8256babb-976x549.png)

<callout type="example">* **Bicycle Brakes**: Transmits force from the brake lever to the brake pads.
* **Steering Systems**: Changes the direction of steering input.</callout>

### How Linkages Work


1. **Input Motion**: A force or movement is applied to one part of the linkage.
2. **Transformation**: The linkage alters the direction, magnitude, or path of the motion.
3. **Output Motion**: The transformed motion is transmitted to another part of the system.
